Focused aim: Define premium/luxe vs mass/consumer beauty packaging requirements and justify segmentation using credible sources.
Key outputs to capture: Definitions; why premium and mass differ; packaging implications; source-backed segmentation rationale.

Focused aim: Identify current common primary packaging materials used in cosmetics and personal care by format.
Key outputs to capture: Materials by bottle/jar/tube/pouch/cap/pump/compact; format-material fit; premium vs mass use.

Focused aim: Identify current common secondary and tertiary packaging materials used in cosmetics.
Key outputs to capture: Folding cartons; rigid boxes; inserts; trays; shippers; cushioning; labels/sleeves; current materials.

Focused aim: Build indicative global price baseline for common packaging materials.
Key outputs to capture: PET; PP; HDPE; LDPE/PE; glass; aluminum; paperboard; corrugated; molded pulp; price source and region/date assumptions.

Focused aim: Build baseline for material density and weight drivers to support lightweighting evaluation.
Key outputs to capture: Density; gram-weight drivers; typical material weight implications; why low density alone is not enough.

Focused aim: Identify material compatibility and safety/regulatory constraints for cosmetic packaging.
Key outputs to capture: Compatibility with oils/fragrance/alcohol/surfactants/actives; migration/contact safety; barrier needs; regulatory references.

Focused aim: Establish how packaging weight affects logistics cost and related transport impacts.
Key outputs to capture: Actual weight vs dimensional weight; freight/logistics cost logic; damage/return impact; emissions if relevant.

Focused aim: Find lightweight materials/technologies used in food and beverage packaging that may transfer to cosmetics.
Key outputs to capture: Lightweighted PET; thin-wall containers; mono-material films; lightweight paperboard; commercial examples and fit.

Focused aim: Find lightweight materials/technologies used in pharma/healthcare packaging that may transfer to cosmetics.
Key outputs to capture: High-barrier plastics; tubes; blister/film learnings; lightweight bottles; safety and compatibility evidence.

Focused aim: Scan lightweight materials from automotive/aerospace/electronics and screen for cosmetic packaging feasibility.
Key outputs to capture: Carbon fiber/titanium/magnesium elimination logic; aluminum; foams; honeycomb; composites; cost/practicality screen.

Focused aim: Research thin-wall and downgauged PET/PP/HDPE packaging as near-term lightweighting routes.
Key outputs to capture: Weight reduction mechanism; material savings; fit for bottles/jars/caps; risks; supplier/commercial examples.

Focused aim: Research foamed and microcellular plastics for lightweight molded packaging parts.
Key outputs to capture: Foamed PP/PET/PE; MuCell-like processes; weight reduction ranges; surface/aesthetic issues; use cases.

Focused aim: Research refill pouches and mono-material flexible films for weight reduction.
Key outputs to capture: PE/PP mono-material pouches; refill model; weight/logistics benefits; limitations; recyclability and barrier.

Focused aim: Research lightweight cosmetic tubes including mono-material and laminate alternatives.
Key outputs to capture: PE/PP tubes; laminate tubes; paper-based tubes; weight/cost/barrier; suitability by product type.

Focused aim: Research lower-GSM, high-stiffness paperboard and lightweight carton solutions for secondary packaging.
Key outputs to capture: GSM reduction; board grades; carton strength; printability; premium vs mass suitability.

Focused aim: Research molded pulp/fiber inserts and protective secondary packaging alternatives.
Key outputs to capture: Plastic tray replacement; molded fiber trays; pulp inserts; cushioning; weight, cost, and appearance trade-offs.

Focused aim: Research barrier-coated paper/fiber materials for primary or secondary cosmetic packaging.
Key outputs to capture: Moisture/oil/oxygen barrier coatings; paper tubes/jars; limitations with wet formulations; recyclability.

Focused aim: Research material-led lightweighting for caps, closures, pumps, and dispensing systems.
Key outputs to capture: Reduced-component pumps; lightweight PP/PE caps; refillable closures; material substitutions; cost and compatibility.

Focused aim: Evaluate PCR/recycled and bio-based materials for weight, cost, feasibility and cosmetic packaging fit.
Key outputs to capture: rPET/rPP/rHDPE; bio-PET/PE; PLA/PHA; weight vs sustainability; cost premiums; technical limitations.

Focused aim: Develop price-first screening and decision matrix for all common and cross-industry lightweight material options.
Key outputs to capture: Keep/maybe/remove material list; effective cost per functional pack; premium/mass fit; primary/secondary fit; final shortlist.
